is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study analyzes pfcrt gene in 98 P. falciparum field isolates.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Sequenced pfcrt codons 72-76 including K76T (position 76 is a target Pfcrt codon).

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives per-site counts of pfcrt alleles among numbered samples (e.g., Manaus 37 samples, 33 S(tct)MNT, 3 CIET).

sub_country_location.evidence: Table 1 breaks frequencies by named towns/states with coordinates: Manaus, Porto Velho, Peixoto de Azevedo/Apiacás, Tabatinga, Iquitos (Loreto).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Collection windows given per site: Manaus 2000-2002, Mato Grosso 1992-1994, Tabatinga July 2001, Iquitos 2001-2002 — all <=3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: All samples collected in Brazil, Peru, and Colombia (Amazon Basin, South America); laboratory strains 7G8 and TM284 also included. No African field samples.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical field study genotyping 98 isolates.

duplicate_risk.evidence: New field collection; no stated consortium overlap, though references prior related collections (Wootton et al.).

supplement.evidence: Per-site pfcrt allele frequencies fully present in Table 1 of the main text.

assessment.summary: Primary study genotyping pfcrt codons 72-76 (including K76T) in 98 P. falciparum field isolates across South American sites with sub-country resolution and adequate temporal binning. However, all samples are from Brazil, Peru, and Colombia — no African field samples — so it fails the African field-sample criterion.
exclusion_reasons: Samples were collected in South America (Brazil, Peru, Colombia), not in an African country.
